author: Mohamed Diomande
name: Anticipatory Transformer V2: MoE Trajectory Routing + Entropy-Weighted Loss
blurb: Builds on SOTA stack (10L, Int5-MLP/Int6-Attn, BigramHash 10240, SmearGate, OrthoInit, MuonWD, SWA, sliding window eval, zstd-22) with three innovations from anticipation geometry: (1) MoE trajectory routing with 10 specialist experts, each mapping 7 hidden-state-derived scalars to per-head attention modulation. Routing via learned centroids, zero-parameter at routing time. Validated: -7.4% perplexity on wikitext-2 (4-way controlled ablation). (2) Entropy-weighted training loss (alpha=0.15) focusing gradient on high-uncertainty tokens. (3) 7-scalar trajectory extraction (commitment, uncertainty, transition_pressure, recovery_margin, phase_stiffness, novelty, stability) from hidden state dynamics. Scalars proven necessary and sufficient for model convergence on 291K ASR data. Total MoE overhead: ~2.5K params, ~5% compute.
notes: Pending H100 validation. All innovations independently toggleable: MOE_TRAJECTORY_ENABLED, ENTROPY_LOSS_ENABLED, TRAJECTORY_ATTN_ENABLED. MoE routing adds ~2.5K params (10 experts x 7->16->8 + 10x7 centroids). Orthogonality penalty (0.01) encourages expert specialization. Scalars recomputed at encoder/decoder boundary for evolved representations.
